description: Command file has no YAML frontmatter block; `name` field is absent so NLPM cannot register or discover this command
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with `name: merge` at the top of the file

description: Command file has no YAML frontmatter block; `description` field is absent so NLPM scoring and auto-classification fail
suggested_fix: Add `description: Finalize worktree work — update docs, commit, merge to main, clean up` to frontmatter

description: Command file has no YAML frontmatter block; `name` field is absent so NLPM cannot register or discover this command
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with `name: prime` at the top of the file

description: Command file has no YAML frontmatter block; `description` field is absent so NLPM scoring and auto-classification fail
suggested_fix: Add `description: Load project context from core ai-context docs, then handle user request` to frontmatter

description: Comment on L6 documents intended usage as `curl -fsSL … | bash` — executes arbitrary remote code without inspection; compromised GitHub repo yields RCE on all users running the one-liner
suggested_fix: Provide a checksum-verified install path; document `curl -O` + `sha256sum -c` + manual review before `bash install.sh`

description: Script downloads a tarball from GitHub (L90) and executes the extracted `setup.sh` directly (L188) without signature or checksum verification — functionally identical to curl-pipe-bash in two steps
suggested_fix: Verify tarball integrity with a published SHA-256 or GPG signature before extracting and executing

description: `$file` (from hook JSON `attached_files[]`) is interpolated directly into a double-quoted echo forming a JSON object; a filename containing `"` or `\` produces malformed JSON and could inject a `"decision":"allow"` field, silently bypassing the security block
suggested_fix: Replace bare echo with `jq -n --arg f "$file" --arg r "Security Alert…" '{"decision":"block","reason":$r}'`

description: `$file_path` originates from JSON `attached_files[]` and is passed to `stat` and `cat` without verifying the path remains within the project root; attacker-influenced input could read arbitrary host files
suggested_fix: Resolve path with `realpath --relative-base "$PROJECT_ROOT"` and reject paths that escape the project directory

description: State file at `/tmp/claude-stop-${SESSION_ID}.state` uses SESSION_ID from untrusted JSON hook input; predictable path enables TOCTOU / symlink attacks in shared `/tmp/`
suggested_fix: Use `mktemp -t claude-stop.XXXXXXXX` and store the resulting path in an env var or side-channel safe location

description: Baseline file at `/tmp/claude-baseline-${SESSION_ID}.numstat` uses SESSION_ID from untrusted hook JSON; a pre-created symlink or file at that path can poison the git-diff baseline, causing the stop hook to under-count new changes
suggested_fix: Use `mktemp` to create a unique temp file and pass the path through a safe session-scoped variable

description: `printf '%b'` in MSG construction interprets backslash escapes from `$FILE_LIST`, which contains filenames from `git diff` output; crafted filenames with `\n` or `\t` can corrupt the JSON reason string sent to Claude Code
suggested_fix: Replace `printf '%b' "$MSG"` with `printf '%s' "$MSG"` to suppress escape-sequence interpretation

description: Command declares no `allowed-tools` field; tools used (Bash, ExitWorktree) are undeclared, making automated tool-permission audits unreliable
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Bash, ExitWorktree, Read]` to frontmatter

description: `$ARGUMENTS` is embedded in the command but no guard handles the case where it is empty; the Pre-flight section proceeds unconditionally
suggested_fix: Add an early check: `If $ARGUMENTS is empty, proceed with no additional context`

description: "Stage relevant files by name" — "relevant" is undefined; no criterion distinguishes relevant from irrelevant files
suggested_fix: Specify: "Stage all files shown in git diff HEAD except .env, credentials, and binary assets"

description: Command declares no `allowed-tools` field; tools used to read @-referenced docs are undeclared
suggested_fix: Add `allowed-tools: [Read]` to frontmatter

description: `$ARGUMENTS` is echoed at the end with no empty-input guard; if called with no arguments the final instruction `Then process the user's request: ` is a dangling imperative
suggested_fix: Add: `If $ARGUMENTS is empty, stop after the confirmation summary and await user input`

description: All shell command blocks contain `[YOUR_TEST_COMMAND]`, `[YOUR_DEPLOY_COMMAND]`, etc. — skill is structurally correct but non-functional until customised; a naive invocation would produce no useful output
suggested_fix: Provide a worked example (e.g., a Node.js deploy) to demonstrate expected substitution; or add a preflight check that aborts with clear guidance if placeholders remain

description: Workflow ends at `Step 5 — Verify` (read the output image) with no explicit confirmation message to the user; no summary of what was done or where the output file lives
suggested_fix: Add a Step 6 Report block: `Done: <operation> applied\n  Output: <path>\n  Dimensions: <WxH>`

description: Workflow ends at Step 6 `Post-process` (copy + delete) with no formal report to the user; final state and chosen variant path are not confirmed
suggested_fix: Add a brief report after cleanup: `Generated: <path>  Size: <WxH>  Variant: N of M`

description: "If none are good enough" (L104) and "Pick the single best variant" (L102) use subjective criteria with no measurable threshold for acceptance
suggested_fix: Define acceptance criteria: e.g., "Reject if character's face shape, color palette, or art style deviates visibly from the primary reference image"

description: Multiple vague intensifiers without definition: "genuinely useful" (L11), "routine tasks" (L22), "real consequences" (L22), "add value" (L17) — each leaves the usage trigger undefined and model-dependent
suggested_fix: Replace each hedge with a concrete example or criterion, e.g., "routine" → "tasks you can answer in one step from training data"

description: Skill ends at Step 5 (create missing files) with no summary to the user of which files were updated and what changed
suggested_fix: Add a Step 6 Report: list each file updated with a one-line summary of the change made

description: "Only update files where the change is meaningful" — "meaningful" is undefined; the model must guess what meets the bar
suggested_fix: Replace with the concrete skip list already in the `When to Skip` section: "Update only when a feature, API contract, directory structure, or status milestone changed"

description: "relevant source files changed" (L29) and "substantial implementation work" (description) use undefined thresholds
suggested_fix: Tie to the 50-line threshold already in the skill: "If relevant source files (matching the patterns in Step 2) changed"

description: Sections 2 (Architecture), 4 (Coding Standards), 5 (Testing), and 6 (Privacy/Security) are entirely commented-out HTML comments with no active rules; a project that installs this template gains no guidance in those four areas
suggested_fix: Uncomment and populate at least one concrete rule per section, or add a prominent TODO marker that fails NLPM scoring until filled in
